text: Does the coupled Burgers-swept-KdV system decompose a localized Gaussian wave packet (amp=4, sigma=1.5) in v (with u=0) into a train of >=2 well-separated solitons each with amplitude >=0.8 by T=6, while keeping mass(v) drift <8%?

method: Fourier pseudospectral on [-15,15] with Nx=256; explicit RK4 on full RHS; NO 2/3 dealiasing; periodic BC.
bank_use_rationale: E1 is simplest baseline per progressive-complexity discipline. Reject central FD entries because central FD cannot resolve v_xxx accurately at Nx=256.

rationale: Confirmed BKdV-S1 deep-synthesis prediction: aliasing wall, not RK4 stiffness.

method: Fourier pseudospectral + explicit RK4 + 2/3-rule dealiasing on every nonlinear product (v^2, u*v, u*u_x, v*v_x); also dealias fields pre-derivative.
bank_use_rationale: Single-component upgrade: add only 2/3 dealiasing. Rejected: Lax-Friedrichs over-diffusion, IFRK4 overflow, IMEX-CN (NL-CFL violated at amp=4 per kb-gardner-G4/cubicTerm). BKdV-S1 deep-synthesis (depth=3) directly motivates 2/3-rule.

rationale: E2 reached T=6 with mass exactly conserved. Soliton train forms cleanly at t=1-3 (2-3 well-separated peaks, amps 1.4-2.4). By t=6, peak count increases to 14 with v_min=-2.4 reflecting BKdV cross-coupling (u_t -= d_x(3v^2+v_xx) per BKdV-S5). Phenomenon target met but want dt-convergence check given amp=4 is unprecedented (bank caps at amp=3).
Single-component dt-convergence check at dt=2.5e-5 (4x smaller) to guard against kb-gardner-cubicTerm-tightens-nonlinearCFL warning at unprecedented amp=4.

method: Same as E2 (Fourier pseudospectral + RK4 + 2/3 dealiasing), with dt reduced 4x to 2.5e-5 for convergence verification.
bank_use_rationale: Single-component change: only dt. Rejected escalations: switching to IMEX-CN (kb-gardner-G4 explicitly blows up at amp=3, amp=4 is 1.5x worse), hyperviscosity (BKdV-S4 deep depth=3 warns nu_h reshapes attractor — physics knob not numerical), Nx doubling (BKdV-S4 violates co-constraint nu_h*k_max^16). The diagnostic suite (peak count + amplitude + mass + sup-norm + finiteness) addresses kb-general-finiteness-not-accuracy and kb-general-massConservation-insufficient-diagnostic.

diagnostics.convergence_with_E2: Early times (t=1,2,3) identical to E2; late times (t=4-6) qualitatively similar but quantitatively diverge (v_max_t6 5.19 vs 3.87 in E2). Both runs satisfy phenomenon target.

rationale: E3 corroborates E2's early-time soliton train decomposition exactly (identical v_max/v_min through t=3). Both runs show 2 peaks >=0.8 at t=1, 2 at t=2, 3 at t=3 — clean soliton-train decomposition. Late-time divergence (small at t=4-5, larger at t=6) consistent with BKdV-S5 deep-synthesis: the m=0 manifold is non-invariant for sech^2/Gaussian ICs, system explores chaotic dispersive regime where small dt differences accumulate. Both runs satisfy phenomenon target: >=2 peaks each >=0.8, mass drift exactly 0%. Selecting E3 as final answer (smaller dt = more conservative).
Phenomenon target satisfied by both E2 and E3. Selecting E3 (smaller dt, more conservative) as final. Mass drift 0%, n_peaks >=0.8 = 14 at t=6 (>>=2 required), all finite, no blow-up.
